channel.title: Business News - Markets reports and financial news from Sky

image.title: Business News - Markets reports and financial news from Sky

channel.description: Sky business news provides up to the minute reports on markets, share prices and the world economy, alongside expert business commentary.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Sky UK. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Supermarkets 'encouraged to cap food prices' - but bosses hit out at '1970s-style ⁠price controls'
item.description: The government has moved to distance itself from reported efforts to introduce price caps on key supermarket products, such as eggs, bread and milk, in return for the easing of some regulations.
item.media:description: A trolley full of groceries in a supermarket.

item.title: UK waters down Russian oil sanctions
item.description: The UK government has watered-down sanctions on Russia, allowing diesel and jet fuel from Russian crude oil to enter the UK if it is refined in other countries.
item.media:description: Man working on top of a tanker wagon at a Rosneft oil terminal. Pic: Reuters

item.title: West Africa-focused oil cash shell to float in London in £30m boost to IPO market | Mark Kleinman blog
item.media:description: Paternoster Square, where the London Stock Exchange is based. Pic: iStock

item.title: Pending tax claims against Trump to be dropped, says Justice Department
item.description: The US government has agreed to permanently drop any past tax claims against President Donald Trump, his family and companies.
item.media:description: U.S. President Donald Trump speaks during a roundtable focused on tax cuts in Las Vegas, Nevada, U.S., April 16, 2026. REUTERS/Evan Vucci

item.title: World's 'most expensive' high-speed rail line to be slower and cost more
item.description: A high-speed train line between London and Birmingham will be more expensive, take longer to make and go slower than previously announced.
item.media:description: HS2 staff walk through the Chilterns tunnel at West Hyde, Hertfordshire, as the HS2 project continues. Pic: PA

item.title: Job vacancies fall to lowest level in five years - as unemployment rate rises
item.description: The UK's jobless rate has ticked up to 5% and the pace of basic wage growth has eased further as the country faces down a fresh energy-led price shock.

item.title: Company to replace 'lower-value human capital' with AI
item.description: Standard Chartered bank could cut more than 7,500 jobs as it seeks to replace "lower-value human capital" under a technology and artificial intelligence (AI) drive.
item.media:description: Undated handout photo issued by Finsbury of Standard Chartered bank's London office. The banking giant has laid out plans to hand cash back to shareholders as it became the latest lender to reveal higher profits. The Asia-focused bank launched a one billion US dollar (£800 million) share buyback and plans to return around five billion dollars (£3.95 billion) to shareholders over the next three years. Issue date: Friday February 23, 2024.

item.title: Elon Musk loses lawsuit against OpenAI
item.description: Elon Musk has lost his lawsuit against OpenAI and its chief executive Sam Altman.

item.title: No need for Bank of England rate rise this year, IMF says
item.description: The Bank of England will not need to raise interest rates this year to combat the effects of rising energy costs, according to the world's lender of last resort.
item.media:description: People walk in front of the Bank of England. Pic: AP

item.title: Ryanair sees flat fare headwinds from Iran war
item.description: Ryanair has warned investors that annual profits look set to be pressured by effects from the US-Iran war.
item.media:description: File photo dated 02/09/22 of a Ryanair Boeing 737-8AS passenger airliner comes in to land at Stansted Airport in Essex. Ryanair has revealed around 63,000 of its passengers saw their flights cancelled during last week's air traffic control failure which caused widespread disruption across the industry and left thousands of passengers stranded overseas. In its August traffic update, the Irish carrier said more than 350 of its flights were cancelled on August 28 and 29 due to the air traffic contr
